description: Facing limper(s): isolate-raise / over-limp(call) / fold, by limper count + hero position. Iso ranges value-skewed; over-limp reserved for speculative hands in multiway. Live-specific.

actions.combos: 66+, A9s+, KTs+, QTs+, JTs, ATo+, KJo+, A5s

entries.rationale: Never limp behind — isolate to play heads-up in position with hands that dominate a weak limping range (66+, suited aces/Broadways, ATo+), sizing up to punish the dead money; the small pairs and suited connectors instead over-limp, taking cheap multiway flops where their set/straight implied odds beat trying to isolate (doc 01 §9).

The Button is the best seat to isolate from, so you widen the iso considerably (55+, suited gappers, offsuit Broadways) because guaranteed position lets you realize equity against a capped limper; the tiny pairs and weak suited hands over-limp instead, keeping the pot small and multiway where their implied odds — not raw equity — pay off (doc 01 §9).

With two limpers the pot likely goes multiway even after a raise, so you tighten the isolation to hands that fare well against several callers (77+, suited aces/Broadways) and size larger (4bb + 1bb per limper); more speculative pairs and connectors shift to over-limping, since 3-way-plus pots reward set-mining and drawing over thin isolation (doc 01 §9, §11).
